S.text: Today's a multifocal contact lens fitting — you mentioned trouble with reading up close now?
Right, I can see far fine with my contacts but I'm holding my phone at arm's length.
The far vision seems good right off.

The 20/25 line is clear, the next one's soft. Yeah, that row's clear. The smallest one's a little blurry but I can read the one above it. Both the same now? How long to get used to them? Okay, I'll stick with it. Thanks, doc.

O.text: Right eye -1.00 sphere, left eye -1.25 sphere, minimal cylinder both eyes.
For a multifocal I'll set the trials at base curve 8.6, diameter fourteen0.3, with a low add profile to start— no, given your add is a full +2, I'll go with the medium add profile.
Base curve 8.6 mm, diameter 14.3 mm, medium add OU.
Right eye centration good, coverage complete, movement0.3 millimeters, nicely centered which matters a lot for multifocals since decentration blurs both distance and near.
Left eye also well centered, good coverage.
Multifocal over-refraction is a balance — I check distance and near together.

That's a good near result for a first fit — near acuity roughly 20/25 equivalent, and it'll improve as you adapt. The over-refraction nudged the right from -1.00 to -1.25, so yes, both landed at -1.25 distance with a +2 add.

A.text: Presbyopia, yes, happens to all of us.

P.text: Give it one to two weeks of consistent wear.
Come back in a week; some people I fine-tune the add or balance the eyes.
